description: Config.version (src/config.rs:18) is required in the on-disk schema but never read. It's a trap: it looks like a migration knob but does nothing. Two options: (a) use it - Config::load rejects versions greater than the current schema with a helpful 'your bl is too old' error, and documents the bump protocol; or (b) drop the field entirely with a serde default for back-compat so existing configs still load. Pick (a) since this audit is about forward-proofing, not deleting future-proofing knobs. Add a SCHEMA_VERSION const in config.rs, compare on load, fail with BallError::Other on mismatch (too-new only; too-old = our struct still parses it).
title: Wire up or remove Config.version
